prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arr, me hearties! Avast ye, why is the sky blue? Well, it be 'cause o' the scatterin' o' light. Ye see, when the sun beamin' down upon us, it sends out colors o' the rainbow. An' as these rays o' light travel through our atmosphere, they be scattered about by tiny bits o' dust an' droplets in the air. An' among 'em all, the blue light waves bein' scattered the most, thus fillin' our skies with the beautiful azure hue we know an' love. So there ye go, me mateys. The sky be blue 'cause o' the scatters o' light waves! Shiver me timbers, but that be some powerful stuff, eh?
